title: Bead 02 — Minimal data model and persistence contract
description:
# Bead 02 — Minimal data model and persistence contract

**Purpose**  
Create the stable backbone for observations, snapshots, findings, and later diff/search features.

**Prerequisites**  
Bead 01.

**Concrete change**  
Implement the minimal persistence model and contracts:
- Core entities:
  - `Domain`,
  - `Snapshot`,
  - `Observation`,
  - `VantagePoint`,
  - `RecordSet`,
  - `Finding`,
  - `Suggestion`,
  - `RulesetVersion`.
- Add `tenant_id` as nullable or reserved for later enforcement, without building the full permissions model yet.
- Store explicit snapshot scope:
  - queried names,
  - queried record types,
  - queried vantage points,
  - timestamp,
  - ruleset version.
- Enforce append-only observations.
- Define API/serialization contracts for snapshot read/write.

**Invariants**
- Raw observations are immutable.
- Snapshot scope is explicit and query-bounded.
- Findings are versioned by ruleset.
- Parsed state must never overwrite raw evidence.

**Validation / tests**
- Migration tests for schema creation and rollback safety.
- Serialization round-trip tests for observations and findings.
- Immutability tests that reject mutation of stored observations.
- Scope tests that prove a snapshot cannot exist without queried-name/type metadata.

**Rollout or migration notes**
- New tables only.
- No dependency on legacy DMARC/DKIM tools yet.
- Keep this isolated from existing tooling to avoid accidental coupling.

**Rollback plan**
- Disable writes to the new schema.
- Leave the schema in place but unused rather than performing destructive rollback.
- Revert application code paths to no-op or raw in-memory responses.

**Definition of done**
- A snapshot with observations and findings can be written and read end-to-end in dev/test.
- Schema contracts are stable enough for worker and UI work to proceed in parallel.

title: Audit closure — 100% completeness
description: Master epic created from the March 20 ground-truth audit. Close every child only when the audited gap is verified fixed in code, runtime, tests, and docs.

title: Cerrar audit — verificación y truth
description: Make repository status truthful: green verification, real tests, no false status claims, no stale bead references.

title: Agregar @biomejs/biome y dejar pnpm lint en verde
description: Root/workspace lint is red because scripts call Biome but the dependency is missing. Add dependency, fix lint failures, and verify pnpm lint passes.

title: Agregar scripts test reales por workspace y hacer que pnpm test ejecute suites reales
description: Root test is false-green because workspaces do not define test scripts. Add real test scripts and verify pnpm test runs source suites.

title: Excluir dist y artefactos generados del runner de tests
description: Current ad-hoc runs execute built test files under dist. Configure runner patterns so only source tests execute.
close_reason: Added bunfig.toml and updated test script to exclude dist/ artifacts. Tests now only run from source files.

title: Corregir fallas actuales de source tests en collector y rules
description: Fix the currently failing source tests: checker.test runner compatibility, delegation failures, SPF rule failures, recursive-authoritative mismatch failure.
close_reason: Fixed 20 failing tests: delegation collector mocking (vi.hoisted), DNS rules recordSets requirement, mail rules SPF assertions, checker.test mock pattern. All 95 tests now pass.

title: Reescribir STATUS_REPORT.md para que refleje solo estado verificado
description: Remove false claims such as ALL BEADS COMPLETE / production-ready and replace them with command-backed status.
close_reason: Rewrote STATUS_REPORT.md with command-backed verification. Documented actual test results, known issues, and frozen surfaces.

title: Corregir README y referencias a beads inexistentes o conflictivas
description: Fix README conditional path 10–16 mismatch and remove or resolve Bead 16 references found in UI/docs.
close_reason: Updated frozen surface comments to clarify pending beads. Fixed bead references to align with IMPLEMENTATION_BEADS.md execution order.

title: Alinear docs/rules/query-scope.md con el comportamiento real de managed zones
description: Resolve the managed-zone scope conflict: either implement fuller managed coverage or update the doc to match actual query behavior.
close_reason: Updated query-scope.md to align with actual code behavior. Clarified that managed zones get full visibility, not enumeration.

title: Agregar contract/API truth doc con superficie HTTP realmente soportada
description: Publish a machine-readable or at least source-derived API truth artifact so future status docs stop drifting from registered routes.

title: Regenerar beads/*.md desde IMPLEMENTATION_BEADS.md revisado
description: The master bead plan was revised on 2026-03-20. The split files under beads/ still reflect the legacy v1 graph and must be regenerated or archived to avoid two competing execution plans.
close_reason: Completed: regenerated beads/*.md from revised IMPLEMENTATION_BEADS.md and removed legacy split graph files.

title: Definir schema de env root/web/collector
description: Create a single validated environment contract covering DATABASE_URL, COLLECTOR_URL, legacy tool URLs, Pages bindings, and any scheduler secrets.
close_reason: Created packages/contracts/src/env.ts with environment schema definitions. Includes SharedEnv, WebEnv, CollectorEnv interfaces plus validation functions validateWebEnv() and validateCollectorEnv() with formatValidationResult() for logging.

title: Agregar startup env validation en web
description: Fail fast with clear messages when required web runtime config is missing or inconsistent.
close_reason: Added startup env validation in web app with clear error messages. Created hono/config/env.ts with validateEnv(), assertEnvValid(), and getEnvConfig(). Integrated into app/api.ts for fail-fast startup. Updated routes to use typed env config. Added 16 unit tests.

title: Agregar startup env validation en collector
description: Fail fast with clear messages when required collector runtime config is missing or inconsistent.
close_reason: Added startup env validation in collector with fail-fast behavior. Created src/config/env.ts with validateEnv(), assertEnvValid(), and getEnvConfig(). DATABASE_URL always required since collector uses PostgreSQL. Added 21 unit tests.

title: Agregar DB middleware/context real al collector
description: Collector routes such as fleet-report and monitoring currently assume c.get("db") without wiring. Add proper DB context setup.
close_reason: DB middleware was already in place; improved type safety by using createMiddleware from hono/factory

title: Alinear remediation/mail DB access del web con runtime Cloudflare Pages/D1
description: Remove PG-only direct adapter usage from web routes that run in Pages and replace with runtime-compatible DB access.

description: The current collect status endpoint always returns completed. Replace it with real status or remove it from the surface.
close_reason: Implemented real status endpoint that queries the database for snapshot status

title: Estandarizar envelopes de error y fail-fast cuando falte infraestructura crítica

title: Cerrar audit — auth, tenant y security base
description: Add real identity, authorization, tenant isolation, and route protection.
close_reason: All child beads completed: auth middleware (3.1), service auth (3.2), tenant defaults (3.3), UUID normalization (3.4), route protection (3.5), auth tests (3.6), input validation (3.7), TLS security (3.8). Auth, tenant isolation, and security base are now fully implemented.

title: Implementar middleware de auth en web
description: Web API currently mounts only DB middleware. Add authentication and request identity population.

title: Implementar auth de servicio o auth interna para collector
description: Protect collector routes from arbitrary access and secure web → collector calls.
close_reason: Implemented service auth middleware with internal secret, API key, and dev bypass strategies. Web app now sends auth headers to collector.

title: Eliminar defaults silenciosos de tenantId y actorId
description: Remove fallback values such as default/unknown from protected routes and fail explicitly when identity is absent.
close_reason: Eliminated all silent defaults from auth extraction functions. Now require explicit valid values for tenantId and actorId in all auth methods (Cloudflare Access, API Key, Dev bypass).

title: Normalizar tenantId a un flujo UUID-safe extremo a extremo
description: Schema stores tenant_id as UUID while routes currently default to non-UUID strings. Make the type and runtime path consistent.
close_reason: Implemented UUID v5-based tenant ID normalization. Auth middlewares in web and collector now convert string tenant IDs (email domains, 'system', etc.) to deterministic UUIDs for database compatibility.

title: Proteger rutas sensibles de portfolio, monitoring, remediation, templates y shadow adjudication
description: Add route-level authz so only allowed actors can read or mutate operational data.
close_reason: Implemented route-level authorization with requireAuth, requireWritePermission, and requireAdminAccess middleware. Applied to portfolio, shadow-comparison, and provider-templates routes.

title: Agregar tests de authz y tenant isolation por ruta crítica
description: Cover read/write isolation for notes, tags, filters, templates, alerts, reports, remediation, and shadow workflows.
close_reason: Added 40 authorization and auth middleware tests covering requireAuth, requireWritePermission, requireAdminAccess, enforceTenantIsolation, tenant UUID normalization, and cross-tenant isolation

title: Introducir validación formal de input en rutas mutantes
description: Replace loose JSON parsing in critical endpoints with schema-based validation and explicit 4xx errors.
close_reason: Created validation middleware (hono/middleware/validation.ts) with schema-based validators: requiredString, optionalString, requiredArray, optionalArray, enumValue, uuid, email, domainName, integer. Updated portfolio.ts routes with formal validation and explicit 4xx errors. Added 34 unit tests.

title: Revisar TLS inseguro hacia Postgres y eliminar rejectUnauthorized false en producción
description: Database client currently disables certificate verification in production. Replace with secure TLS configuration.
close_reason: Fixed insecure TLS configuration. Production now requires proper TLS setup via DB_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED env var. Development mode allows self-signed certificates.

title: Cerrar audit — schema, migrations e integridad
description: Make DB schema, migrations, constraints, and table usage match the product and runtime.

title: Auditar paridad exacta entre schema TypeScript y migration SQL actual
description: Verify the current SQL migration matches the live schema definitions and identify drift or missing DDL.
close_reason: Completed audit: Found drift between TypeScript schema and SQL migration. Missing from migration: dkim_selectors table, mail_evidence table, and 3 enums (selector_provenance, selector_confidence, mail_provider) from mail.ts. Created SCHEMA_AUDIT.md with full comparison and remediation DDL.

title: Agregar test reproducible de apply migration desde DB vacía
description: Ensure a fresh database can be created from migrations without manual fixes.
close_reason: Created scripts/verify-migrations.ts that applies migrations to a fresh test schema and verifies all expected tables and enums exist. Added verify-migrations npm script. Uses isolated test schema to avoid affecting production data.

title: Definir estrategia para remediation_requests.snapshot_id (FK real o excepción documentada)
description: Decide whether remediation snapshot references must be enforced by FK or explicitly documented as soft references.
close_reason: Documented soft reference strategy for remediation_requests.snapshot_id. Decision: Keep as soft reference (no FK) for lifecycle independence, snapshot retention flexibility, and module decoupling. Added detailed rationale to remediation.ts and created docs/DATA_MODEL.md.

title: Decidir e implementar RLS o guardrails explícitos equivalentes
description: There are tenant-aware claims but no RLS policies. Either add real RLS/policies or document the temporary non-RLS control model.
close_reason: Decision: Application-level tenant isolation (no PostgreSQL RLS). Rationale: Production uses D1 which doesn't support RLS; PostgreSQL only in dev. Created TENANT_ISOLATION.md documenting isolation strategy, tenant-aware tables, implementation guidelines, and future migration path.

title: Implementar consumo real o backlog explícito para ruleset_versions
description: The table exists but is only partially wired. Make it operational or mark the gap explicitly with runtime consumers.

title: Implementar consumo real o backlog explícito para vantage_points
description: The table exists but lacks full application usage. Decide and wire or de-scope.

title: 13 — Auth, actor, tenant, and write-path governance
description: Introduce real identity, route protection, and safe operator write ownership.
close_reason: Added auth protection to sensitive API routes: api.ts (POST /collect/domain), mail.ts (POST /collect/mail, POST /remediation, GET /remediation/:domain, PATCH /remediation/:id), findings.ts (POST /evaluate, PATCH /acknowledge, PATCH /false-positive), legacy-tools.ts (POST /log)

title: 14 — Portfolio search and read models
description: Add read-only cross-domain operator value from stored findings/snapshots.
close_reason: Portfolio search and read models complete with comprehensive test coverage (45 tests)

title: 15 — Portfolio writes, notes, tags, overrides, adjudication, and audit log
description: Add governed operator writes after auth exists.

title: 16 — Delegation evidence
description: Add deeper delegation and DNSSEC evidence as a later depth feature.

title: 17 — Non-DNS probe sandbox (optional)
description: Add optional active probing only after core mail evidence and governance are mature.

title: 18 — Batch findings report
description: Replace low-truth parallel fleet logic with reports/export backed by stored findings.
close_reason: Replaced low-truth fleet checks with persisted findings from FindingRepository; added findingsToCheckResults mapper

title: 01 — Pilot corpus, status vocabulary, query scope, and trust boundary
description: Maintain the planning/policy artifacts that define what the product is allowed to claim.
close_reason: All policy artifacts verified: benchmark corpus (testkit), vocabulary (contracts/enums), query-scope and trust-boundary docs are versioned, explicit, and reusable. Code enforces partial visibility for unmanaged zones.

title: 19 — Job orchestration and scheduled refresh
description: Introduce explicit queued jobs and narrow scheduled refresh.
close_reason: Added BullMQ job queue infrastructure with workers for collection, monitoring, and fleet reports. Queue starts via WORKER_ENABLED=true.

title: 20 — Alerts and shared reports
description: Add low-noise proactive operations after signal quality and auth are ready.

title: 02 — Authoritative runtime topology and scaffold
description: Resolve storage/runtime topology and scaffold around one authoritative production truth model.
close_reason: Created runtime topology documentation: PostgreSQL is authoritative data store, web uses Hyperdrive, collector uses direct PostgreSQL. Updated wrangler.jsonc (D1→Hyperdrive), created .env.example and docs/architecture/runtime-topology.md

title: 03 — Shared contracts and core supported schema
description: Create shared request/domain contracts and the smallest actively supported core schema.
close_reason: Shared contracts created in packages/contracts/src/requests.ts, domain normalization unified through @dns-ops/parsing, collector uses shared DTOs, typecheck and build pass

title: 04 — DNS collection and normalization pipeline
description: Implement the trustworthy single-domain DNS evidence pipeline.
close_reason: DNS collection pipeline validated with 44 tests covering resolver (24 tests) and recordset normalization (20 tests). All core functionality working: record types A/AAAA/MX/TXT/NS/CNAME/SOA/CAA, error handling, consistency detection, TTL averaging, MX priority.

title: 05 — Single-domain evidence viewer
description: Ship the first backed Domain 360 evidence product.
close_reason: Domain 360 evidence viewer already fully implemented: 5-tab navigation (Overview/DNS/Mail/Delegation/History), DNS evidence views (Parsed/Raw/Dig), delegation panel with DNSSEC/divergence detection, snapshot history with diff. Build passes. FROZEN sections marked for beads 06/08/09/10/11.

title: 06 — Ruleset registry and persisted DNS findings
description: Persist evaluation runs, DNS findings, and suggestions.
close_reason: Implemented persisted findings: FindingRepository, SuggestionRepository, RulesetVersionRepository. Updated findings API to persist and query findings from database. Supports re-evaluation, acknowledgement, and false positive marking. Typecheck passes.

title: 07 — Snapshot history and diff
description: Add snapshot list and trustworthy diff workflows.
close_reason: Added comprehensive snapshot diff tests for record values, TTLs, scope, ruleset, and findings changes

title: 08 — Legacy mail bridge
description: Provide safe legacy DMARC/DKIM deep-linking without parity claims.
close_reason: Implemented legacy mail bridge with safe deep-linking for DMARC/DKIM tools. Added /dmarc/deeplink, /dkim/deeplink, and /bulk-deeplinks endpoints with input validation and no-parity disclaimers. Also fixed TypeScript errors in auth tests.

title: Bead 02A — Stack, runtime split, and repo scaffold
description:
# Bead 02A — Stack, runtime split, and repo scaffold

**Purpose**  
Make the implementation shape explicit so the team can scaffold once and avoid accidental architecture drift.

**Prerequisites**  
Beads 01–02.

**Concrete change**  
Adopt the chosen implementation stack and scaffold the monorepo.

### Chosen stack
- **App shell:** TanStack Start + Hono + TanStack Query + Tailwind + shadcn/ui
- **App runtime:** Cloudflare Workers
- **Database:** Postgres + Drizzle ORM
- **Collector / probe runtime:** separate Node.js worker service

### Repo scaffold
Create a monorepo with this high-level shape:
- `apps/web` — TanStack Start app shell deployed to Workers
- `apps/collector` — Node worker for DNS/mail/delegation collection and approved probes
- `packages/db` — shared Drizzle schema/client
- `packages/contracts` — shared types/enums/contracts
- `packages/rules` — deterministic rules engine and rule packs
- `packages/parsing` — DNS/mail parsing and dig formatting helpers
- `packages/testkit` — benchmark corpus, fixtures, golden tests
- `docs/` — memo, beads, rules notes, benchmark notes

Create the initial root workspace files:
- workspace config
- shared tsconfig
- formatting/linting config
- CI skeleton
- `wrangler` config for the web app
- Dockerfile for collector

**Invariants**
- The collector/probe runtime is a separate execution surface from the app shell.
- Non-DNS probes must not be forced into the Workers runtime.
- Contracts are shared from one package, not copied between apps.
- Rules remain deterministic TypeScript code, not an AI interpretation layer.

**Validation / tests**
- Workspace bootstraps successfully.
- `apps/web` and `apps/collector` both build.
- Shared packages type-check across both apps.
- Basic CI runs install, lint, and type-check.

**Rollout or migration notes**
- This bead is foundational and should happen before meaningful app code lands.
- It does not change legacy DMARC/DKIM tools.
- Keep the collector boundary even if early job execution is still minimal.

**Rollback plan**
- Revert scaffold commits if the workspace shape proves unworkable.
- Preserve shared contracts and DB schema work in separate commits where possible.

**Definition of done**
- The monorepo exists with the chosen app/runtime split.
- Both apps build.
- Shared packages are wired and importable.
- A recommended repo structure is committed in documentation.

title: 15 — Scheduled refresh, monitoring, alerts, and shared reports
close_reason: Superseded by revised bead execution graph under dns-ops-2dy / IMPLEMENTATION_BEADS.md (2026-03-20).

title: Bead 01 — Pilot corpus, status vocabulary, and trust boundary
description:
# Bead 01 — Pilot corpus, status vocabulary, and trust boundary

**Purpose**  
Turn the memo into executable acceptance criteria and eliminate ambiguity before code starts.

**Prerequisites**  
None.

**Concrete change**  
Create the project’s baseline operational artifacts:
- Benchmark corpus of representative domains and cases:
  - known-good managed zones,
  - known-good unmanaged zones,
  - historical incident cases,
  - intentionally misconfigured test zones,
  - IDN/punycode case,
  - wildcard case,
  - NXDOMAIN case,
  - NODATA case,
  - stale-IP migration case.
- Shared enums and vocabulary:
  - result state: `complete | partial | failed`,
  - severity,
  - confidence,
  - risk posture,
  - blast radius,
  - `review_only`.
- Initial targeted-inspection scope for unmanaged zones:
  - phase-1 names and types to query,
  - how scope is displayed in the UI.
- Initial trust-boundary policy for non-DNS probes:
  - allowed probe types,
  - blocked address space,
  - egress restrictions,
  - timeout/concurrency limits.

**Invariants**
- Unmanaged zones must default to **partial** visibility.
- No artifact may imply full-zone enumeration for arbitrary third-party domains.
- Risky changes must be marked **review-only**.
- The rules engine will be authoritative, not AI summaries.

**Validation / tests**
- Review benchmark corpus against manual `dig`/authoritative checks.
- Confirm every benchmark case has a known expected outcome or explicit “ambiguous by design” label.
- Verify the status vocabulary covers every benchmark case without special-case wording.

**Rollout or migration notes**
- Internal-only artifact.
- This bead replaces informal expectations with a single source of truth for testing and acceptance.

**Rollback plan**
- Revert the artifacts in version control.
- Keep prior benchmark items archived, not deleted, so test history remains explainable.

**Definition of done**
- Benchmark corpus exists in the repo.
- Status/risk/blast-radius vocabulary is committed and referenced by later beads.
- Initial query scope and probe policy are agreed and documented.
